subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank MPO, UN/OCHA/UNMISS, RJMEC, Freedom House, RSF, ICG, FEWS NET/IPC, UNICEF) + international press.
Data limitation: official statistics are very weak (no completed census) and most figures are external estimates; the 2025 oil-shock and currency collapse make economic readings highly uncertain.

Standout drags: the 2018 peace deal on the brink (Machar's March 2025 house arrest and treason trial), a ~30% 2025 economic contraction on oil-pipeline disruption, hyperinflation, and a sixth straight year of catastrophic flooding with famine risk.

economic.notes: A single-commodity oil economy in collapse, routed through war-torn Sudan, with hyperinflation.

fiscal_state.notes: Almost entirely oil-funded (>90% of revenue), with revenues evaporating during the 2025 export disruption and ~80% below the poverty line. (Al Jazeera; ICG; World Bank)

monetary_independence.notes: A nominally independent central bank overwhelmed by oil-shock currency collapse and deficit monetization. (World Bank/IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The South Sudanese pound is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: A hyperinflationary surge as oil exports stalled and the pound collapsed in 2025; figures are uncertain but extreme. (ICG; World Bank MPO)

banking_stability.notes: A shallow, dollar-scarce system strained by fiscal collapse and devaluation. (World Bank)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Single-commodity oil dependence routed entirely through Sudan, exposed to that war (a Feb 2025 pipeline breakdown). (Al Jazeera; ICG)

institutional.notes: Weak, politicized institutions, near-absent data, and a censored press.

rule_of_law.notes: Weak/politicized institutions, arbitrary NSS arrests, and the politically charged Machar prosecution; 'Not Free'. (Freedom House 2025; UN experts)

statistical_integrity.notes: Very weak - no completed census, with figures largely external estimates. (World Bank)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Hollowed by fiscal crisis (unpaid salaries) and patronage; even UNMISS cut spending in late 2025. (UNMISS; World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: Pervasive censorship, NSS intimidation, and outlet closures. (RSF; Freedom House 2025)

political_social.notes: A fractured unity government on the edge of renewed civil war, with deep ethnic cleavages.

political_stability.notes: Machar's arrest/trial has fractured the unity government, with armed clashes nationwide and elections again postponed to Dec 2026 - a possible relapse into full-scale war. (UN experts; ICG; RJMEC)

civil_liberties.notes: Arbitrary detention, suppression of dissent, and shrinking civic space. (Freedom House 2025; RSF)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A strained host of returnees/refugees fleeing Sudan's war amid its own collapse. (UNHCR/OCHA)

social_cohesion.notes: Deep Dinka-Nuer and inter-communal cleavages, with the Nuer White Army's March 2025 Nasir assault triggering the crisis. (Al Jazeera)

demographics.notes: Very young and high-fertility but with a severe human-capital deficit from conflict and collapsed services. (UN/World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A destabilizing, transactional regional actor under an arms embargo, dependent on Sudan for oil transit.

alliance_reliability.notes: Backed at times by Uganda and engaged with IGAD/AU, but a transactional actor dependent on Sudan for oil transit. (ICG; UN)

conflict_involvement.notes: Internal armed conflict plus deep exposure to Sudan's civil war via the oil pipeline. (Al Jazeera; ICG)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Under a UN arms embargo and targeted individual sanctions; not comprehensively sanctioned as a state. (UN Security Council)

physical_practical.notes: Catastrophic flooding, a collapsing health system with famine-level food insecurity.

climate.notes: A sixth consecutive year of catastrophic flooding (1M+ affected); among the most climate-vulnerable states. (UN News; OCHA; FEWS NET)

healthcare.notes: A collapsing, aid-dependent system - the country's longest cholera outbreak (100,000+ cases) and millions of children acutely malnourished. (UNICEF; WHO)

infrastructure.notes: Minimal roads/power/water, flood-prone and undeveloped. (World Bank; OCHA)

crime_safety.notes: Widespread armed violence, inter-communal raiding, and conflict insecurity. (UN experts; ACLED-cited)

disaster_insurance.notes: Effectively nonexistent - risk borne by aid/households. (inference)

summary: The world's youngest state, teetering on the edge of renewed civil war: First VP Riek Machar's March 2025 house arrest and subsequent treason trial have fractured the 2018 peace deal, with UN experts and RJMEC warning in late 2025 of a possible relapse into full-scale conflict. Its oil-dependent economy (>90% of revenue), forced to export through war-torn Sudan, contracted around 30% in 2025 amid pipeline disruption, evaporating revenue, and a hyperinflationary pound collapse. A sixth straight year of devastating floods, the longest-ever cholera outbreak, and famine-level food insecurity compound the catastrophe. Official data is sparse and elections are again postponed to December 2026; the outlook is clearly negative.
